NCT03105128.name: ADVANCE
NCT03105128.group: Moderate-severe CD (biologic-naive or -exposed), risankizumab 600 mg IV induction vs placebo (12 wk)
NCT03105128.snippet: Source: DHaens G et al. Lancet 2022;399:2015-2030 (ADVANCE).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 850 adults with moderate-to-severe Crohns disease (CDAI 220-450) with intolerance/inadequate response to conventional or biologic therapy randomized 2:2:1 double-blind to risankizumab 600 mg IV (n=336), 1200 mg IV (n=339), or placebo (n=175) induction at weeks 0, 4, 8.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Clinical Remission at Week 12
evidence.text: Clinical remission (CDAI <150) at week 12: risankizumab 600 mg 152/336 (45%) vs placebo 60/175 (25%); odds ratio 2.49 (95% CI 1.70 to 3.63; P<0.0001). Co-primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Endoscopic Response at Week 12
evidence.text: Endoscopic response (SES-CD reduction >=50%) at week 12: risankizumab 600 mg 135/336 (40%) vs placebo 21/175 (12%); OR 6.40 (95% CI 3.80 to 10.80; P<0.0001). Co-primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization stratified by biologic failure and baseline CDAI. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo IV infusions. D3 LOW - 91% completion of induction. D4 LOW - blinded central endoscopy adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 16 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female aged >=18 to <= 80 years, or minimum age of adult consent according to local regulations, at the Baseline Visit. Where locally permissible, participants 16 to < 18 years of age who meet the definition of Tanner stage 5 for development at the Baseline Visit.~* Diagnosis of CD for at least 3 months prior to Baseline.~* Confirmed diagnosis of moderate to severe CD as assessed by stool frequency (SF), abdominal pain (AP) score, and Simple Endoscopic Score for...

NCT03104413.name: MOTIVATE
NCT03104413.group: Moderate-severe CD biologic-refractory, risankizumab 600 mg IV induction vs placebo (12 wk)
NCT03104413.snippet: Source: DHaens G et al. Lancet 2022;399:2015-2030 (MOTIVATE).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 618 adults with moderate-to-severe CD who had failed or were intolerant of >=1 biologic therapy randomized 2:2:1 double-blind to risankizumab 600 mg IV (n=206), 1200 mg IV (n=205), or placebo (n=207) induction weeks 0, 4, 8.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Clinical Remission at Week 12
evidence.text: Clinical remission (CDAI <150) at week 12: risankizumab 600 mg 103/206 (42%) vs placebo 38/206 (19%); odds ratio 3.15 (95% CI 2.01 to 4.95; P<0.0001). Co-primary endpoint met in biologic-refractory population.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Endoscopic Response at Week 12
evidence.text: Endoscopic response at week 12: risankizumab 600 mg 59/206 (29%) vs placebo 23/206 (11%); OR 3.07 (95% CI 1.74 to 5.44; P<0.0001).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization stratified by prior biologic failure number and CDAI baseline. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo schedule. D3 LOW - 90% completion. D4 LOW - central blinded endoscopy. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 16 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female aged >=18 to <= 80 years, or minimum age of adult consent according to local regulations, at the Baseline Visit. Where locally permissible, participants 16 to < 18 years of age who meet the definition of Tanner stage 5 for development at the Baseline Visit.~* Confirmed diagnosis of CD for at least 3 months prior to Baseline.~* Crohn's disease activity index (CDAI) score 220 - 450 at Baseline.~* Confirmed diagnosis of moderate to severe CD as assessed by s...

NCT03105102.name: FORTIFY
NCT03105102.group: CD wk12 responders, risankizumab 360 mg SC Q8W vs placebo maintenance (52 wk)
NCT03105102.snippet: Source: Ferrante M et al. Lancet 2022;399:2031-2046 (FORTIFY).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 542 CD patients achieving clinical response to risankizumab IV induction (ADVANCE/MOTIVATE week 12) re-randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to SC maintenance with risankizumab 360 mg Q8W (n=141), 180 mg Q8W (n=145), or placebo (n=164) for 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Clinical Remission at Week 52
evidence.text: Clinical remission (CDAI <150) at week 52: risankizumab 360 mg SC 71/141 (52%) vs placebo 55/164 (40%); odds ratio 1.79 (95% CI 1.14 to 2.80; P=0.0054). Co-primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - Endoscopic Response at Week 52
evidence.text: Endoscopic response at week 52: risankizumab 360 mg 67/141 (47%) vs placebo 25/164 (22%); OR 4.14 (95% CI 2.37 to 7.22; P<0.0001). Durable endoscopic benefit in maintenance.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central re-randomization stratified by induction dose and biologic failure. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo SC. D3 LOW - 85% completion at 52 wk. D4 LOW - blinded central endoscopy. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 16 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Participants who have entered and completed Study M16-006 or Study M15-991 or other AbbVie risankizumab Crohn's disease study.~* Participants have completed the study M16-006 or M15-991 and have achieved clinical response.~* Sub-Study 4:~  * Participants receiving maintenance treatment in Sub-study 3 and willing to comply with the requirements of Sub-study 4, including self-administration of sub-cutaneous injections using the on-body injector (OBI).~ * Participant has rece...

title: RapidMeta Gastroenterology | Risankizumab in Crohn's Disease v1.0
